table user_profile

table favorite_facility

-- Brings the schema in line with spec §2-5 (allergy memory is opt
-- §2-12 (an allergy verdict must record how confident the match w
--
-- Why each column exists:
--
-- user_profile.remember_allergies
-- Allergies are a session input by default. They only reach this d
-- when the user explicitly asks us to remember them, and the flag
-- makes that promise checkable rather than merely stated.
--
-- allergy_ingredient.normalized_key / confidence
-- "Ibuprofen", "ibuprofen", "IBUPROFEN (200mg)" are one ingredient
...
-- normalised key is what we compare; `confidence` records whether
--
-- name is not.
COMMENT 'Opt-in. FALSE means allergies live only in the browser ses
ADD COLUMN normalized_key VARCHAR(255) NULL
ADD COLUMN confidence VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UNKNOWN'
-- Matching happens on the normalised key, not the raw text the use
ADD COLUMN alias VARCHAR(100) NULL
-- facility_id now carries a provider namespace (spec §4-3): `faci
COMMENT 'Provider-namespaced id, e.g. facility:nmc:C1110693. Never

function git

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# One-time setup for a fresh clone. Safe to re-run.
#
# ./bin/setup.sh
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
echo "▸ Enabling the pre-commit secret guard"
# `pnpm install` below sets this too, via bin/install-hooks.mjs, for anyone who never runs
# this file. Done here as well so the guard is on before we touch anything else — and with
# plain git, because node may not be installed yet.
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
...
chmod +x .githooks/pre-commit
echo "▸ Creating .env from .env.example — fill in your keys before running the backend"
echo "▸ .env already exists, leaving it alone"
(cd frontend && pnpm install)
cat <<'EOF'
1. Fill in .env
LLM_API_KEY → your OpenAI-compatible endpoint
3. Run the backend (http://localhost:8080)
cd frontend && pnpm dev
EOF

What must never regress: a refused location says so out loud, * an alert rather than silence, and an empty result is a sentence — not a bl * like the app broke. * * `FacilityMap` needs the Naver SDK, which is `FacilityMap.test.tsx`'s busines * stub that echoes its props. ... */ return { ...actual, resolveLocation: resolveLocationMock, fetchFacilities: fet {props.caption && {props.caption}} latitude: 37.5, resolveLocationMock.mockReset() }) expect(screen.queryByTestId('stub-notice')).not.toBeInTheDocument() it('renders a fetch failure as an alert, not silence', async () => { it('says in words that nothing was found, instead of showing a bare map', asyn })

import OpenAI from 'openai'
import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
import { FALLBACK_DISCLAIMER, describeSendFailure, openai, parseAnswer } from '.
/**
* The bug this file exists for.
*
* `baseURL` was `'/api/v1'`. The backend had 275 passing tests, every one of the
* and the chat had never once run in a browser. The SDK concatenates — `new UR
* and a relative base makes that a relative string, which single-argument `URL`
* constructor is fine; `buildURL` throws on the first request, inside `send()`,
* So the screen said "something went wrong" and the network tab stayed empty.
*
...
* A typecheck cannot see this. Only something that builds the URL can — and it
expect(url.pathname).toBe('/api/v1/chat/completions')
*/
it('trusts an explicit retryable: true the same way', () => {
/**
urgency: { level: 'routine', title: 'T', message: 'M', reasonCodes: [], actions:
expect(answer.summary).toBe('Take paracetamol.')
expect(answer.uiActions).toEqual([])
expect(answer.answerId).toBe('local-fallback')
})

-- Brings the schema in line with spec §2-5 (allergy memory is opt
-- §2-12 (an allergy verdict must record how confident the match w
--
-- Why each column exists:
--
-- user_profile.remember_allergies
-- Allergies are a session input by default. They only reach this d
-- when the user explicitly asks us to remember them, and the flag
-- makes that promise checkable rather than merely stated.
--
-- allergy_ingredient.normalized_key / confidence
-- "Ibuprofen", "ibuprofen", "IBUPROFEN (200mg)" are one ingredient
...
-- normalised key is what we compare; `confidence` records whether
--
-- name is not.
COMMENT 'Opt-in. FALSE means allergies live only in the browser ses
ADD COLUMN normalized_key VARCHAR(255) NULL
ADD COLUMN confidence VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UNKNOWN'
-- Matching happens on the normalised key, not the raw text the use
ADD COLUMN alias VARCHAR(100) NULL
-- facility_id now carries a provider namespace (spec §4-3): `faci
COMMENT 'Provider-namespaced id, e.g. facility:nmc:C1110693. Never

-- Base64url HIRA identifiers can exceed the prior 120-character limit once provider-prefixed.
-- This new migration deliberately leaves V2 unchanged: Flyway validates applied checksums at startup.
ALTER TABLE favorite_facility
MODIFY COLUMN facility_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
COMMENT 'Provider-namespaced id, e.g. facility:nmc:C1110693. Never a name.';

function git

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# One-time setup for a fresh clone. Safe to re-run.
#
# ./bin/setup.sh
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
echo "▸ Enabling the pre-commit secret guard"
# `pnpm install` below sets this too, via bin/install-hooks.mjs, for anyone who never runs
# this file. Done here as well so the guard is on before we touch anything else — and with
# plain git, because node may not be installed yet.
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
...
chmod +x .githooks/pre-commit
echo "▸ Creating .env from .env.example — fill in your keys before running the backend"
echo "▸ .env already exists, leaving it alone"
(cd frontend && pnpm install)
cat <<'EOF'
1. Fill in .env
LLM_API_KEY → your OpenAI-compatible endpoint
3. Run the backend (http://localhost:8080)
cd frontend && pnpm dev
EOF
